Title: Natural variation in the transcription factor REPLUMLESS contributes to both disease resistance and plant growth in Arabidopsis

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes IAA-Asp accumulation, bacterial effector expression/translocation, susceptibility to Pst DC3000, and restrains growth
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher transcript abundance in rpl-4 at 6 hpi
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; gh3.2 mutant phenotyping; pathogen assay; effector gene expression; effector translocation assay
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GH3.2 is upregulated in rpl mutants during infection. 2. gh3.2 mutation reduces bacterial accumulation and effector expression/translocation. 3. gh3.2 mutants show larger rosettes and greater fresh weight, supporting a role in growth-defense tradeoff.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Reducing GH3.2 activity may improve resistance and growth by lowering IAA-Asp
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: GH3.2 promoter used in Y1H and LUC assays

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely contributes to elevated IAA-Asp and susceptibility when derepressed in rpl mutant
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher transcript abundance in rpl-4 at 6 hpi
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GH3.3 is upregulated in rpl mutants after infection. 2. RPL directly binds the GH3.3 promoter and represses its activity in vitro and in vivo. 3. GH3.3 is implicated as part of the RPL-controlled auxin-conjugation module affecting defense.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target to reduce IAA-Asp-associated susceptibility
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: GH3.3 promoter used in Y1H, EMSA, and LUC assays

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Transcript elevated in rpl mutants, but gh3.6 mutant showed no significant defense phenotype in this study
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher transcript abundance in rpl-4 at 6 hpi
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; gh3.6 mutant phenotyping; Y1H negative for direct binding
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GH3.6 expression increases in rpl mutants after infection. 2. gh3.6 loss did not significantly alter bacterial accumulation in this study. 3. Direct binding of RPL to GH3.6 promoter was not supported in Y1H.

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Col-0; rpl-4; rpl-1; RPL-FLAG/rpl-4; chi mutant
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Flavonols inhibit auxin transport and thereby limit plant growth
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes flavonol accumulation and restrains growth; repressed by RPL to enhance biomass
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in rpl mutants and reduced in RPL overexpression line under non-infected conditions
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Elevated in rpl-1 and rpl-4 leaves without infection
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: No bacterial infection; standard growth conditions
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; flavonol quantification; EMSA; chi mutant growth phenotyping
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CHI is upregulated in rpl mutants under normal growth conditions. 2. RPL directly binds the CHI promoter and represses expression. 3. Elevated CHI in rpl mutants is associated with increased flavonol levels and reduced growth. 4. chi mutant displays larger rosette diameter and fresh weight, supporting CHI as a growth-restraining node.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Reducing CHI expression/activity may promote growth, but flavonoid-related defense/metabolic consequences should be considered
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Flavonoid deficiency may affect other specialized metabolites and defense in some species

Regulation_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Col-0; Ler; natural accession Ha-P-13 variant analyzed
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Col-0; Ler; rpl-4; rpl-1; RPL-FLAG/rpl-4; natural accessions for GWAS
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: BEL1-like TALE homeodomain (BLH) transcription factor; putative POX domain; K233T natural variant in POX domain
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: IAA-Asp promotes bacterial virulence effector expression and pathogen proliferation; flavonols inhibit auxin transport and restrain growth
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of RPL increases susceptibility to Pst DC3000 and reduces rosette size/fresh weight; overexpression decreases bacterial growth and increases biomass
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key positive regulator coupling defense and growth; directly links auxin conjugation and flavonol biosynthesis to disease resistance and biomass
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional repression via direct promoter binding
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses GH3.2 and GH3.3 promoter activity directly; represses CHI directly; GH3.6 transcript elevated in mutant but direct promoter binding not supported in Y1H
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: auxin homeostasis/conjugation; flavonoid biosynthesis; auxin transport indirectly
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Pathogen infection by Pseudomonas syringae pv. tomato DC3000 induces RPL expression
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Transcript peaked at 6 h post infection; previously reported highest in flowers and inflorescences
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaves during bacterial infection; also developmental expression in flowers and inflorescences
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pst DC3000 infection; exogenous IAA-Asp or IAA treatment; normal growth conditions without infection
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS; T-DNA mutants rpl-4 and rpl-1; overexpression/complementation line RPL-FLAG/rpl-4; RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; IAA-Asp quantification by LC-MS/MS; Y1H; EMSA; dual-luciferase assays; pathogen growth assays; effector translocation and virulence gene expression assays
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GWAS identified RPL as the causal transcription factor underlying natural variation in bacterial resistance. 2. RPL positively regulates both immunity and growth in Arabidopsis. 3. During infection, RPL represses GH3 genes, especially GH3.2/GH3.3, reducing IAA-Asp accumulation and thereby suppressing bacterial effector expression/translocation and pathogen proliferation. 4. In healthy plants, RPL represses CHI, lowering flavonol accumulation and indirectly promoting auxin transport gene expression and growth. 5. A natural K233T variant shows weaker promoter-binding activity and is associated with reduced resistance.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: RPL homologs are promising targets for breeding or engineering crops with improved disease resistance and growth/yield without major tradeoff; natural functional alleles and promoter-binding capacity should be considered
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Paper suggests RPL can improve resistance and growth simultaneously; exact switching mechanism and possible environment-specific costs remain unresolved
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Natural nonsynonymous SNP A698C causes K233T substitution and weaker DNA binding; variant present in 13.4% of ecotypes. RPL likely acts mainly as a transcriptional repressor. Upstream regulator remains unknown.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV35S constitutive promoter used for RPL-FLAG overexpression in Arabidopsis; GH3.2pro:LUC and GH3.3pro:LUC reporters in protoplast assays
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome resequencing (SRA: SRP344532); RNA-seq (Illumina NovaSeq 6000, accession NA in text)
